title: Ghost Context Injection via Reentrancy in _detachAllContexts
description: The _detachAllContexts loop (and the inline loop in REP15._beforeTokenTransfer) captures the attached context array length once before iterating. During the onExecDetachContext callback inside _detachContext, a malicious controller can re-enter attachContext and append a new context beyond the captured loop bound. After token transfer the recipient inherits the ghost context which can be immediately locked for up to 365 days.
impact: Griefing: buyer's token is transfer-locked for up to 365 days. Requires malicious controller with setApprovalForAll or approve() from the token owner.
recommendation: Add a reentrancy guard flag in REP15Storage that blocks attachContext re-entry during _detachAllContexts, or snapshot the attached context array to memory before the loop.

title: Transfers Not Guarded in REP15PausableUpgradeable
description: REP15PausableUpgradeable guards delegation and context operations but not token transfers. Transfers while paused still call _detachAllContexts which fires onExecDetachContext callbacks on external controller contracts.
impact: External controller calls fire during emergency pause, potentially violating the expected no-state-changes-while-paused invariant.
recommendation: Override _beforeTokenTransfer in REP15PausableUpgradeable to call _requireNotPaused() for non-mint transfers.

title: Wrong Revert Error for execDetachContext on Non-Attached Context
description: execDetachContext calls _detachContext(checkReadyForDetachment: true). For a non-attached context, readyForDetachmentAt is zero, triggering REP15NotRequestedForDetachment instead of the correct REP15NonexistentAttachedContext.
impact: Misleading error message for off-chain integrators. No fund loss.
recommendation: In _detachContext, check attachment before readyForDetachmentAt when checkReadyForDetachment is true.

title: Authorization Check After Delegation State Query in startDelegateOwnership
description: startDelegateOwnership checks delegation.isActive() before the caller authorization check. Unauthorized callers can infer whether an active delegation exists by observing the revert reason. This information is already public via getOwnershipDelegatee().
impact: No practical exploit. Cosmetic ordering issue.
recommendation: Move _isApprovedOrOwner check before delegation state check.

title: Redundant _attachedContexts Storage in Enumerable Extension
description: REP15Upgradeable and REP15EnumerableUpgradeable both maintain _attachedContexts and _attachedContextsIndex mappings in separate ERC-7201 storage slots, updated in parallel via the virtual hook chain. This doubles the SSTORE count on every attach and detach.
impact: Increased gas cost only. No security impact.
recommendation: Consider making the base-class _attachedContexts internal and allowing enumerable extensions to reuse it, or provide a virtual internal getter that _detachAllContexts calls.
